title: Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs scrubs Instagram account
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stefon Diggs deleted all posts from his Instagram account, though his Story featured recent GQ cover photos and gym videos.
Diggs's $6 million signing bonus with the Patriots becomes fully guaranteed on March 13, and he has a $20.6 million base salary.
Diggs faces assault and strangulation charges with a pretrial hearing set for April 1, following an alleged incident in December.

title: Get one-way flights for as low as $79 during JetBlue’s spring sale
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue's spring sale offers one-way flights starting at $79 for travel between April 14 and June 10.
The sale ends on March 5 at 11:59 p.m. EST, with blackout dates from May 21-26.
Discounted flights include destinations like Daytona Beach, Nashville, Los Angeles, and international routes like London.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in March
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Hulu adds three Oscar nominees in March: 'The Secret Agent,' 'It Was Just an Accident,' and 'Sentimental Value.'
Netflix debuts the Peaky Blinders movie 'The Immortal Man' and 'One Piece' season 2 in March.
HBO Max introduces new shows such as 'Rooster' starring Steve Carell on March 8 and revives 'The Comeback' on March 22.

title: Husband and wife charged with throwing rocks at passing cars in Southwick
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Celestine Robinson and David Ducharme charged with 17 felonies for throwing rocks at cars in Southwick, MA.
Robinson charged additionally with DUI; breathed alcohol level of 0.127, with state limit at 0.08.
Incidents occurred over two days in February; Robinson's bail set at $1,500, Ducharme held without bail.

title: With deadline looming, still no word on security funding for World Cup in Foxborough
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials await $7.8 million security funding decision for World Cup at Gillette Stadium, potentially blocking matches.
Boston World Cup 2026 committee lacks clarity on who is responsible for funding, creating tensions with Foxborough officials.
Massachusetts eligible for over $46 million in federal security funds; decision delayed, complicating local World Cup planning.

title: Lamplighter Brewing Co. workers vote to join union
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Lamplighter Brewing Co. workers in Cambridge have voted to unionize under UFCW Local 1445.
The union covers various industries including food, retail, and now includes Lamplighter workers from both Broadway and Cambridge Crossing locations.
Workers aim to negotiate over wages, benefits, health care, and workplace safety in their first contract discussions.

title: Pedestrian hospitalized after being struck by MBTA bus in Norwood
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 35-year-old woman was hit by an MBTA bus in Norwood and hospitalized on Monday.
The incident occurred around 3:45 p.m. on Washington Street; the woman was not in a marked crosswalk.
The victim is in stable condition; MBTA Transit Police are investigating the crash circumstances.

title: A Mass. town voted down a new fire station. The old one just got shut down after the blizzard.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Westwood's Fire Station 1 closed due to structural issues after a blizzard, prompting operations to move across town.
Westwood voters rejected funding a new $38.1 million fire station, leaving the current one in disrepair and closed.
Fire Station 1's conditions include water damage and structural instability, raising long-term safety concerns for the town's fire services.

title: He’s running his 130th marathon at the 130th Boston Marathon
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Alberto Colin from Long Beach is running his 130th marathon at the 130th Boston Marathon.
Colin has participated in the Boston Marathon for 16 consecutive years since his first in 2011.
His marathon milestones include running his 100th and 115th marathons at previous Boston Marathons.

title: A new six-story building could rise where a pizza shop and dentist now stand in Jamaica Plain
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: VinCo Properties proposes a six-story building with 35 apartments and retail space at 3716-3724 Washington St., Jamaica Plain.
The current site, housing Forest Hills Pizza and Forest Hills Dental, would be replaced to create a mixed-use property.
The development, requiring zoning relief, includes 10 parking spaces and plans for sidewalk widening to improve safety.
